# Port target: `@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin`

| | |
|---|---|
| Upstream repo | https://github.com/typescript-eslint/typescript-eslint |
| Submodule | `upstream/typescript-eslint` @ `v8.62.0` (`packages/eslint-plugin`) |
| Baseline npm version | `8.62.0` |
| License | MIT |
| Oxlint native support | none — port target |
| Rules to port | 134 |

> Oxlint has native TypeScript lint coverage for some rules; tracked here as an explicit upstream reference.

## Rules

| # | Rule | Description | Docs |
|---|------|-------------|------|
| 1 | `adjacent-overload-signatures` | Require that function overload signatures be consecutive | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/adjacent-overload-signatures) |
| 2 | `array-type` | Require consistently using either `T[]` or `Array<T>` for arrays | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/array-type) |
| 3 | `await-thenable` | Disallow awaiting a value that is not a Thenable | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/await-thenable) |
| 4 | `ban-ts-comment` | Disallow `@ts-<directive>` comments or require descriptions after directives | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/ban-ts-comment) |
| 5 | `ban-tslint-comment` | Disallow `// tslint:<rule-flag>` comments | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/ban-tslint-comment) |
| 6 | `class-literal-property-style` | Enforce that literals on classes are exposed in a consistent style | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/class-literal-property-style) |
| 7 | `class-methods-use-this` | Enforce that class methods utilize `this` | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/class-methods-use-this) |
| 8 | `consistent-generic-constructors` | Enforce specifying generic type arguments on type annotation or constructor name of a constructor call | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/consistent-generic-constructors) |
| 9 | `consistent-indexed-object-style` | Require or disallow the `Record` type | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/consistent-indexed-object-style) |
| 10 | `consistent-return` | Require `return` statements to either always or never specify values | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/consistent-return) |
| 11 | `consistent-type-assertions` | Enforce consistent usage of type assertions | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/consistent-type-assertions) |
| 12 | `consistent-type-definitions` | Enforce type definitions to consistently use either `interface` or `type` | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/consistent-type-definitions) |
| 13 | `consistent-type-exports` | Enforce consistent usage of type exports | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/consistent-type-exports) |
| 14 | `consistent-type-imports` | Enforce consistent usage of type imports | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/consistent-type-imports) |
| 15 | `default-param-last` | Enforce default parameters to be last | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/default-param-last) |
| 16 | `dot-notation` | Enforce dot notation whenever possible | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/dot-notation) |
| 17 | `explicit-function-return-type` | Require explicit return types on functions and class methods | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/explicit-function-return-type) |
| 18 | `explicit-member-accessibility` | Require explicit accessibility modifiers on class properties and methods | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/explicit-member-accessibility) |
| 19 | `explicit-module-boundary-types` | Require explicit return and argument types on exported functions' and classes' public class methods | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/explicit-module-boundary-types) |
| 20 | `init-declarations` | Require or disallow initialization in variable declarations | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/init-declarations) |
| 21 | `max-params` | Enforce a maximum number of parameters in function definitions | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/max-params) |
| 22 | `member-ordering` | Require a consistent member declaration order | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/member-ordering) |
| 23 | `method-signature-style` | Enforce using a particular method signature syntax | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/method-signature-style) |
| 24 | `naming-convention` | Enforce naming conventions for everything across a codebase | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/naming-convention) |
| 25 | `no-array-constructor` | Disallow generic `Array` constructors | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-array-constructor) |
| 26 | `no-array-delete` | Disallow using the `delete` operator on array values | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-array-delete) |
| 27 | `no-base-to-string` | Require `.toString()` and `.toLocaleString()` to only be called on objects which provide useful information when stringified | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-base-to-string) |
| 28 | `no-confusing-non-null-assertion` | Disallow non-null assertion in locations that may be confusing | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-confusing-non-null-assertion) |
| 29 | `no-confusing-void-expression` | Require expressions of type void to appear in statement position | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-confusing-void-expression) |
| 30 | `no-deprecated` | Disallow using code marked as `@deprecated` | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-deprecated) |
| 31 | `no-dupe-class-members` | Disallow duplicate class members | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-dupe-class-members) |
| 32 | `no-duplicate-enum-values` | Disallow duplicate enum member values | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-duplicate-enum-values) |
| 33 | `no-duplicate-type-constituents` | Disallow duplicate constituents of union or intersection types | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-duplicate-type-constituents) |
| 34 | `no-dynamic-delete` | Disallow using the `delete` operator on computed key expressions | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-dynamic-delete) |
| 35 | `no-empty-function` | Disallow empty functions | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-empty-function) |
| 36 | `no-empty-interface` | Disallow the declaration of empty interfaces | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-empty-interface) |
| 37 | `no-empty-object-type` | Disallow accidentally using the "empty object" type | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-empty-object-type) |
| 38 | `no-explicit-any` | Disallow the `any` type | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-explicit-any) |
| 39 | `no-extra-non-null-assertion` | Disallow extra non-null assertions | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-extra-non-null-assertion) |
| 40 | `no-extraneous-class` | Disallow classes used as namespaces | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-extraneous-class) |
| 41 | `no-floating-promises` | Require Promise-like statements to be handled appropriately | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-floating-promises) |
| 42 | `no-for-in-array` | Disallow iterating over an array with a for-in loop | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-for-in-array) |
| 43 | `no-implied-eval` | Disallow the use of `eval()`-like functions | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-implied-eval) |
| 44 | `no-import-type-side-effects` | Enforce the use of top-level import type qualifier when an import only has specifiers with inline type qualifiers | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-import-type-side-effects) |
| 45 | `no-inferrable-types` | Disallow explicit type declarations for variables or parameters initialized to a number, string, or boolean | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-inferrable-types) |
| 46 | `no-invalid-this` | Disallow `this` keywords outside of classes or class-like objects | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-invalid-this) |
| 47 | `no-invalid-void-type` | Disallow `void` type outside of generic or return types | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-invalid-void-type) |
| 48 | `no-loop-func` | Disallow function declarations that contain unsafe references inside loop statements | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-loop-func) |
| 49 | `no-loss-of-precision` | Disallow literal numbers that lose precision | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-loss-of-precision) |
| 50 | `no-magic-numbers` | Disallow magic numbers | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-magic-numbers) |
| 51 | `no-meaningless-void-operator` | Disallow the `void` operator except when used to discard a value | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-meaningless-void-operator) |
| 52 | `no-misused-new` | Enforce valid definition of `new` and `constructor` | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-misused-new) |
| 53 | `no-misused-promises` | Disallow Promises in places not designed to handle them | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-misused-promises) |
| 54 | `no-misused-spread` | Disallow using the spread operator when it might cause unexpected behavior | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-misused-spread) |
| 55 | `no-mixed-enums` | Disallow enums from having both number and string members | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-mixed-enums) |
| 56 | `no-namespace` | Disallow TypeScript namespaces | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-namespace) |
| 57 | `no-non-null-asserted-nullish-coalescing` | Disallow non-null assertions in the left operand of a nullish coalescing operator | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-non-null-asserted-nullish-coalescing) |
| 58 | `no-non-null-asserted-optional-chain` | Disallow non-null assertions after an optional chain expression | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-non-null-asserted-optional-chain) |
| 59 | `no-non-null-assertion` | Disallow non-null assertions using the `!` postfix operator | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-non-null-assertion) |
| 60 | `no-redeclare` | Disallow variable redeclaration | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-redeclare) |
| 61 | `no-redundant-type-constituents` | Disallow members of unions and intersections that do nothing or override type information | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-redundant-type-constituents) |
| 62 | `no-require-imports` | Disallow invocation of `require()` | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-require-imports) |
| 63 | `no-restricted-imports` | Disallow specified modules when loaded by `import` | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-restricted-imports) |
| 64 | `no-restricted-types` | Disallow certain types | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-restricted-types) |
| 65 | `no-shadow` | Disallow variable declarations from shadowing variables declared in the outer scope | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-shadow) |
| 66 | `no-this-alias` | Disallow aliasing `this` | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-this-alias) |
| 67 | `no-type-alias` | Disallow type aliases | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-type-alias) |
| 68 | `no-unnecessary-boolean-literal-compare` | Disallow unnecessary equality comparisons against boolean literals | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-unnecessary-boolean-literal-compare) |
| 69 | `no-unnecessary-condition` | Disallow conditionals where the type is always truthy or always falsy | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-unnecessary-condition) |
| 70 | `no-unnecessary-parameter-property-assignment` | Disallow unnecessary assignment of constructor property parameter | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-unnecessary-parameter-property-assignment) |
| 71 | `no-unnecessary-qualifier` | Disallow unnecessary namespace qualifiers | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-unnecessary-qualifier) |
| 72 | `no-unnecessary-template-expression` | Disallow unnecessary template expressions | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-unnecessary-template-expression) |
| 73 | `no-unnecessary-type-arguments` | Disallow type arguments that are equal to the default | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-unnecessary-type-arguments) |
| 74 | `no-unnecessary-type-assertion` | Disallow type assertions that do not change the type of an expression | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-unnecessary-type-assertion) |
| 75 | `no-unnecessary-type-constraint` | Disallow unnecessary constraints on generic types | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-unnecessary-type-constraint) |
| 76 | `no-unnecessary-type-conversion` | Disallow conversion idioms when they do not change the type or value of the expression | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-unnecessary-type-conversion) |
| 77 | `no-unnecessary-type-parameters` | Disallow type parameters that aren't used multiple times | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-unnecessary-type-parameters) |
| 78 | `no-unsafe-argument` | Disallow calling a function with a value with type `any` | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-unsafe-argument) |
| 79 | `no-unsafe-assignment` | Disallow assigning a value with type `any` to variables and properties | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-unsafe-assignment) |
| 80 | `no-unsafe-call` | Disallow calling a value with type `any` | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-unsafe-call) |
| 81 | `no-unsafe-declaration-merging` | Disallow unsafe declaration merging | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-unsafe-declaration-merging) |
| 82 | `no-unsafe-enum-comparison` | Disallow comparing an enum value with a non-enum value | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-unsafe-enum-comparison) |
| 83 | `no-unsafe-function-type` | Disallow using the unsafe built-in Function type | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-unsafe-function-type) |
| 84 | `no-unsafe-member-access` | Disallow member access on a value with type `any` | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-unsafe-member-access) |
| 85 | `no-unsafe-return` | Disallow returning a value with type `any` from a function | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-unsafe-return) |
| 86 | `no-unsafe-type-assertion` | Disallow type assertions that narrow a type | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-unsafe-type-assertion) |
| 87 | `no-unsafe-unary-minus` | Require unary negation to take a number | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-unsafe-unary-minus) |
| 88 | `no-unused-expressions` | Disallow unused expressions | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-unused-expressions) |
| 89 | `no-unused-private-class-members` | Disallow unused private class members | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-unused-private-class-members) |
| 90 | `no-unused-vars` | Disallow unused variables | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-unused-vars) |
| 91 | `no-use-before-define` | Disallow the use of variables before they are defined | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-use-before-define) |
| 92 | `no-useless-constructor` | Disallow unnecessary constructors | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-useless-constructor) |
| 93 | `no-useless-default-assignment` | Disallow default values that will never be used | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-useless-default-assignment) |
| 94 | `no-useless-empty-export` | Disallow empty exports that don't change anything in a module file | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-useless-empty-export) |
| 95 | `no-var-requires` | Disallow `require` statements except in import statements | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-var-requires) |
| 96 | `no-wrapper-object-types` | Disallow using confusing built-in primitive class wrappers | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-wrapper-object-types) |
| 97 | `non-nullable-type-assertion-style` | Enforce non-null assertions over explicit type assertions | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/non-nullable-type-assertion-style) |
| 98 | `only-throw-error` | Disallow throwing non-`Error` values as exceptions | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/only-throw-error) |
| 99 | `parameter-properties` | Require or disallow parameter properties in class constructors | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/parameter-properties) |
| 100 | `prefer-as-const` | Enforce the use of `as const` over literal type | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/prefer-as-const) |
| 101 | `prefer-destructuring` | Require destructuring from arrays and/or objects | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/prefer-destructuring) |
| 102 | `prefer-enum-initializers` | Require each enum member value to be explicitly initialized | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/prefer-enum-initializers) |
| 103 | `prefer-find` | Enforce the use of Array.prototype.find() over Array.prototype.filter() followed by [0] when looking for a single result | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/prefer-find) |
| 104 | `prefer-for-of` | Enforce the use of `for-of` loop over the standard `for` loop where possible | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/prefer-for-of) |
| 105 | `prefer-function-type` | Enforce using function types instead of interfaces with call signatures | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/prefer-function-type) |
| 106 | `prefer-includes` | Enforce `includes` method over `indexOf` method | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/prefer-includes) |
| 107 | `prefer-literal-enum-member` | Require all enum members to be literal values | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/prefer-literal-enum-member) |
| 108 | `prefer-namespace-keyword` | Require using `namespace` keyword over `module` keyword to declare custom TypeScript modules | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/prefer-namespace-keyword) |
| 109 | `prefer-nullish-coalescing` | Enforce using the nullish coalescing operator instead of logical assignments or chaining | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/prefer-nullish-coalescing) |
| 110 | `prefer-optional-chain` | Enforce using concise optional chain expressions instead of chained logical ands, negated logical ors, or empty objects | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/prefer-optional-chain) |
| 111 | `prefer-promise-reject-errors` | Require using Error objects as Promise rejection reasons | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/prefer-promise-reject-errors) |
| 112 | `prefer-readonly` | Require private members to be marked as `readonly` if they're never modified outside of the constructor | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/prefer-readonly) |
| 113 | `prefer-readonly-parameter-types` | Require function parameters to be typed as `readonly` to prevent accidental mutation of inputs | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/prefer-readonly-parameter-types) |
| 114 | `prefer-reduce-type-parameter` | Enforce using type parameter when calling `Array#reduce` instead of using a type assertion | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/prefer-reduce-type-parameter) |
| 115 | `prefer-regexp-exec` | Enforce `RegExp#exec` over `String#match` if no global flag is provided | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/prefer-regexp-exec) |
| 116 | `prefer-return-this-type` | Enforce that `this` is used when only `this` type is returned | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/prefer-return-this-type) |
| 117 | `prefer-string-starts-ends-with` | Enforce using `String#startsWith` and `String#endsWith` over other equivalent methods of checking substrings | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/prefer-string-starts-ends-with) |
| 118 | `prefer-ts-expect-error` | Enforce using `@ts-expect-error` over `@ts-ignore` | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/prefer-ts-expect-error) |
| 119 | `promise-function-async` | Require any function or method that returns a Promise to be marked async | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/promise-function-async) |
| 120 | `related-getter-setter-pairs` | Enforce that `get()` types should be assignable to their equivalent `set()` type | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/related-getter-setter-pairs) |
| 121 | `require-array-sort-compare` | Require `Array#sort` and `Array#toSorted` calls to always provide a `compareFunction` | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/require-array-sort-compare) |
| 122 | `require-await` | Disallow async functions which do not return promises and have no `await` expression | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/require-await) |
| 123 | `restrict-plus-operands` | Require both operands of addition to be the same type and be `bigint`, `number`, or `string` | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/restrict-plus-operands) |
| 124 | `restrict-template-expressions` | Enforce template literal expressions to be of `string` type | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/restrict-template-expressions) |
| 125 | `return-await` | Enforce consistent awaiting of returned promises | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/return-await) |
| 126 | `sort-type-constituents` | Enforce constituents of a type union/intersection to be sorted alphabetically | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/sort-type-constituents) |
| 127 | `strict-boolean-expressions` | Disallow certain types in boolean expressions | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/strict-boolean-expressions) |
| 128 | `strict-void-return` | Disallow passing a value-returning function in a position accepting a void function | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/strict-void-return) |
| 129 | `switch-exhaustiveness-check` | Require switch-case statements to be exhaustive | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/switch-exhaustiveness-check) |
| 130 | `triple-slash-reference` | Disallow certain triple slash directives in favor of ES6-style import declarations | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/triple-slash-reference) |
| 131 | `typedef` | Require type annotations in certain places | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/typedef) |
| 132 | `unbound-method` | Enforce unbound methods are called with their expected scope | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/unbound-method) |
| 133 | `unified-signatures` | Disallow two overloads that could be unified into one with a union or an optional/rest parameter | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/unified-signatures) |
| 134 | `use-unknown-in-catch-callback-variable` | Enforce typing arguments in Promise rejection callbacks as `unknown` | [docs](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/use-unknown-in-catch-callback-variable) |
